label: Wait For Condition
description: Poll an expression until it becomes truthy (or a timeout elapses). Differs from `wait`, which sleeps a fixed duration. Useful for: 'wait until job_status == done', 'wait until pod ready', 'wait until queue depth < N'. Reference: n8n's Wait node 'until condition' variant + the n8n/Flowise/Langflow gap analysis (P1 #3).

settings.name: condition
settings.label: Condition (JS expression)
settings.description: Expression evaluated against `input` on each poll. Returns truthy → continue; falsy → wait and re-poll. Same sandbox semantics as the `condition` node.

settings.name: pollIntervalSeconds
settings.label: Poll interval (seconds)
settings.description: Seconds between condition evaluations. Use longer intervals for expensive checks (e.g. mcp_tool calls upstream) — the engine re-evaluates the SAME `input` so the inputs need to be expected to change by your upstream flow design.

settings.name: timeoutSeconds
settings.label: Timeout (seconds)
settings.description: Maximum total wait. If the condition is still falsy when this elapses, the node emits { satisfied: false, timedOut: true } and the workflow continues — downstream `condition` nodes can branch on `{{steps.<id>.timedOut}}`.

settings.name: failOnTimeout
settings.label: Fail on timeout
settings.description: When true, a timeout raises node_error instead of returning `timedOut: true`. Default false so workflows can model the timeout path explicitly.

ai.shortDescription: Poll an expression until it becomes truthy, with a timeout.
ai.whenToUse: Async waits where the condition depends on external state changes (job completion, resource readiness, rate-limit reset). Use the simpler `wait` node for fixed sleeps.
ai.examplePrompt: wait until the deployment is ready, polling every 10 seconds for up to 5 minutes

outputAssertions.name: shape_complete
outputAssertions.errorMessage: wait_for did not emit the required output shape (satisfied/polls/durationMs/timedOut).
